heroHeadline: Dallas Revises Police ICE Policy Under Abbott's $90M FIFA Threat; 150+ National Protests Set for April 25 Against Warehouse Detention
heroSubtitle: Dallas Police Chief Comeaux bowed to Gov. Greg Abbott's threat to withhold $90 million in state and FIFA World Cup security funding on April 24, 2026, removing the ban on prolonged ICE detention holds — while retaining a bar on immigration-status-only stops. Simultaneously, over 150 events are planned nationwide on April 25 to oppose DHS's $38B plan to convert warehouses into 100,000-bed detention camps. The U.S. Senate's $70B ICE reconciliation resolution advances to the House (June 1 deadline). FY2026 ICE custody deaths stand at 29 — a 22-year record; acting Director Todd Lyons departs May 31.
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
